title: Lesson Quiz: Types of Credit and Loans
description: Assessment of the concept that Credit cards, personal loans, student loans, mortgages.

questions.prompt: Which type of loan is most closely connected to buying a home?

choices.feedback: Correct. Mortgages are loans commonly used to purchase homes or property.
Student loans are connected to education expenses.
Credit cards are not the main borrowing tool for buying a home.
A fee is not a type of home loan.

questions.prompt: Different types of credit exist because borrowing needs are not all the same.

choices.feedback: Different borrowing tools are designed for different financial purposes.
Correct. Borrowing options should be matched to the specific need.

questions.prompt: Which borrowing option is most closely connected to education costs?

choices.feedback: Mortgages are associated with property purchases.
Credit cards are not the main borrowing tool designed for education costs.
Correct. Student loans are borrowing tools associated with education expenses.
Sales tax is not a type of educational borrowing.

questions.prompt: Why is it important to match the type of borrowing to the stated need?

choices.text: Because all borrowing options serve the exact same purpose
choices.feedback: Different borrowing options often fit different needs.

choices.text: Because the strongest borrowing option is the one that fits the actual financial purpose
choices.feedback: This is the strongest reasoning, but the correct answer in this set is placed elsewhere.

choices.text: Because borrowing should ignore long-term responsibility
Because the strongest borrowing option is the one that fits the actual financial purpose
choices.feedback: Correct. Strong borrowing decisions compare the need to the purpose of the loan type.

questions.prompt: Choosing a borrowing option only because it is available is always the strongest financial strategy.

choices.feedback: Borrowing decisions should be based on fit, cost, and responsibility, not just availability.
Correct. Availability alone does not make a borrowing choice strong.
